channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Maryland senator to visit El Salvador to check on mistakenly deported man
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D) said he is traveling to El Salvador on Wednesday to check on the status of a deported legal resident.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KREX 5

item.title: 3 men, 1 teen facing multiple charges in Louisiana for riding horses in Walmart
item.description: Three men and a teenage boy are facing charges after they rode horses through a Louisiana Walmart and posted a video of their adventure on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran's president accepts resignation of 2015 nuclear deal figure as UN atomic chief due in Tehran
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran 's president formally approved the resignation of one of his vice presidents who served as Tehran's key negotiator in its 2015 nuclear deal with world powers, just as the head of the United Nations' nuclear watchdog was due to arrive in the Islamic Republic on
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and NASSER KARIMI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Children on playgrounds targeted by drug runners with candy-colored 'trash cans,' DEA agent warns
item.description: Some drug dealers are moving toward selling drugs in small, brightly colored capsules called "trash cans." The containers have been found near schools and on playgrounds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in shooting at Dallas high school is in custody, officials say
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A suspect in a shooting at a Dallas high school that wounded four students and drew a heavy police response to the campus has been taken into custody, school district officials announced Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Oklahoma City bombing was 30 years ago. Some survivors worry America didn’t learn the lesson
item.description: Thirty years after a truck bomb detonated outside a federal building in the nation’s heartland, deep scars still remain from the deadliest homegrown attack on U.S. soil.
item.dc:creator: Sean Murphy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The White House is starting a new media policy that restricts wire services' access to the president
item.description: The White House has put forward a new media policy that sharply curtails access to Donald Trump by the news agencies that serve media outlets around the world.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's lawyer says no proof of MS-13 affiliation
item.description: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's attorney, Benjamin Osorio, joined "CUOMO" for a live exclusive interview to discuss his client's status.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Kim Kardashian will testify in a trial over a 2016 robbery that targeted her in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Kim Kardashian will testify in person at an upcoming trial over a 2016 heist in Paris in which armed robbers allegedly tied her up and locked her in a bathroom while they stole millions of dollars' worth of jewelry, her lawyer said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Harvard stands to lose $2.2 billion in federal funding. Researchers fear science will suffer
item.description: CAMBRIDGE, Mass. (AP) — In a high-stakes standoff, President Donald Trump's administration says it will freeze $2.2 billion in federal research grants for Harvard University, which is pushing back on demands for changes to campus policy.
item.dc:creator: BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden alleges Trump has 'taken a hatchet' to Social Security in his first post-presidency speech
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former President Joe Biden warned on Tuesday that President Donald Trump has “taken a hatchet” to Social Security, weighing in on a critical issue for millions of Americans as the 82-year-old Democrat briefly returned to the national stage.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gambian ex-soldier convicted at US trial of torturing suspected backers of a failed 2006 coup
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former member of Gambia's military was convicted in federal court Tuesday of torturing five people accused of involvement in a failed coup against the West African country's longtime dictator nearly 20 years ago, capping a rare prosecution in the United States for torture committed a
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two Venezuelan illegal aliens charged with kidnapping, torturing, attempting to kill Washington state woman
item.description: ICE placed detainers on two illegal aliens from Venezuela in Washington who are accused of kidnapping, torturing, shooting and trying to kill a woman back in January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man charged with arson at Pennsylvania governor's home struggled with mental health, brother says
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man accused of setting fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s mansion over the weekend had struggled with mental illness, twice being treated at a psychiatric hospital, as his life unraveled dramatically in the past few years, his brother said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, KRON4

item.title: Video shows ICE agents shatter car window during Massachusetts arrest: attorney
item.description: Federal immigration agents are accused of shattering a car window in Massachusetts while detaining a Guatemalan man on Monday in a caught-on-camera incident – despite his attorney saying they had the wrong person.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, Fox 5

item.title: Hertz says some customers' personal info exposed in breach
item.description: While the company did not say how many may have been impacted, they did identify the type of information that may have been exposed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: US judge presses Trump administration on its refusal to return Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge said Tuesday that she will order sworn testimony by Trump administration officials to determine if they complied with her orders to facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No jurors picked on first day of Harvey Weinstein’s #MeToo retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As jury selection began Tuesday in Harvey Weinstein’s New York City rape retrial, some prospective jurors made clear they couldn't be fair in judging the one-time Hollywood mogul-turned-#MeToo pariah.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indiana Supreme Court sets May execution for man convicted of killing police officer
item.description: A man convicted of killing an Indiana police officer during a foot chase 24 years ago will be executed next month, the state Supreme Court ruled Tuesday. Benjamin Ritchie shot Beech Grove Police Officer Bill Toney in September 2000.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC Council sues Adams administration over ICE office on Rikers Island
item.description: ICE used to have a presence on Rikers; however, the city’s 2014 sanctuary law effectively banned ICE operations in its jails.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, NewsNation

item.title: Man allegedly held captive by stepmother in Connecticut releases first public statement
item.description: In his first public statement, the man allegedly held captive in his home for over 20 years by his stepmother says he's been getting stronger as he begins the process of reclaiming his life.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, KRON4

item.title: Lawyer for detained Palestinian activist says client is relieved he hasn't been moved from Vermont
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A Palestinian man arrested at a Vermont immigration office during an interview about finalizing his U.S. citizenship is relieved that he hasn't been moved out of state and thankful for those supporting him, his attorney said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida man confronts neighbor accused of shooting protected bird, video shows
item.description: A heated confrontation between a Florida homeowner and his neighbor over the shooting of a protected bird has prompted outrage in the community.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tennessee mom tried to hire hit man to kill former TV anchor ex-husband, put him ‘underground’: docs
item.description: Angelia "Angie" Sullivan has been arrested in connection with a murder-for-hire plot against her ex-husband, seeking to have him killed to access trust fund money.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why every MLB player will wear the same number Tuesday
item.description: Every MLB player, regardless of team or position, will be wearing the exact same jersey number on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Big law spends top dollar as Trump takes aim at firms
item.description: A series of new agreements brokered between President Trump and major law firms suggests a new price tag for those looking to dodge executive orders targeting the legal profession.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Jennifer Hudson hosts Remarkable Women for Hollywood experience
item.description: The 2025 Remarkable Women trip continues to dazzle as the winners took in a true taste of Tinseltown on Monday.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: American pastor's kidnapping in South Africa fueled by soaring armed robbery incidents in recent years
item.description: Tennessee pastor Josh Sullivan was kidnapped in South Africa on April 10, and his captors are reportedly demanding a ransom amid rising armed robbery rates.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill O'Reilly believes Trump has several tariff deals already done
item.description: Bill O'Reilly recently spoke with President Trump and believes the president has several tariff deals already done.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: Elephants at San Diego park form protective circle during earthquake
item.description: Watch: Elephants at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park surrounded the younger elephants in a protective "alert circle" when a 5.2 magnitude earthquake struck the region Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, KRON4

item.title: Immigration judges sue over mass firings at DOJ
item.description: A group of recently appointed immigration judges who were fired by mass layouts have filed a lawsuit against the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Video shows mother pick up baby and run during Southern California earthquake
item.description: A quick-thinking San Diego County mother saved her baby from a 5.2 magnitude earthquake in eastern San Diego County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KRON4

item.title: FEMA denies Washington state disaster relief from bomb cyclone, governor says
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency has denied Washington state's request for emergency relief funds to help repair an estimated $34 million in damage from a deadly bomb cyclone storm system in November, according to Gov. Bob Ferguson.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vance fumbles Ohio State's NCAA football championship trophy as Buckeyes visit the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President JD Vance ended the Ohio State football team's visit to the White House on Monday by fumbling the team's national championship trophy.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump wants Congress to end daylight saving time
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday urged Congress to “push hard for more Daylight at the end of a day” in his latest dig at the semiannual changing of clocks.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Popular soup, bowl products may be contaminated; USDA issues public health alert
item.description: The USDA has issued a nationwide public health alert warning consumers that some popular soup and bowl products could be contaminated with wood.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WGN 9
